topics: Introduction and Relevance of Biomedical Signal Processing; Biomedical Signals - ECG
Aliasing and Sampling Theorem; Elementary Signals; Classification of Discrete Signals - Energy and Power Signals, Periodic and Aperiodic

Even and Odd Signals; Discrete Time Systems - Properties of Discrete Systems; LTI System - Impulse Response of a Linear Time-Invariant System, Convolution, Correlation, and Difference Equation Representation of Discrete Systems
Fourier Analysis for Continuous Time Processes - Synthesis of an ECG Signal Using Pure Sinusoid; Frequency Domain Analysis of Discrete Time Signals: Discrete Fourier Series
Discrete Time Fourier Transform - Discrete Fourier Transform Properties
Fast Fourier Transform - FFT Algorithms (Radix 2) - Decimation in
Spectrum Analysis of Bio Signals - Introduction to Parametric and Non-Parametric Methods; Z Transform - ROC
Initial Value Theorem; Rational Z Transform; Poles and Zeros; LTI System Function; Inverse Z Transform - Long Division, Partial Fraction Expansion Method, Residue Method, Convolution Method
Types of Digital Filters; Comparison with Analog Filters; FIR Filter Design Using Fourier Series - Use of Window Functions

IIR Digital Filters; Analog Filter Approximations - Butterworth Approximation; Frequency Transformation Techniques - Impulse Invariant Transformation, Matched Z Transform Technique, Bilinear Transformation; Suggestion on Project Topics - Students are asked to form groups of maximum five members. Each group should identify a project on the areas specified below and choose suitable data base. Develop a methodology to implement the project. Develop the algorithm and code for the project; Areas of Projects Topics: Filtering for Removal of Artefacts - Implementation of Time Domain Filters and Frequency Domain Filters for Removal of High Frequency, Low Frequency, and Periodic Artefacts; Event Detection in Biomedical Signals - ECG: QRS Wave Detection
P Wave and T Wave Detection

PPG: Dicrotic Notch Detection; Heart Sound Detection; EEG Rhythm Detection; Frequency Domain Characterisation of Biomedical Signal - Power Spectrum Density Estimation of Biomedical Signals; Wave Shape and Waveform Complexity Analysis - Normal and Ectopic Beat
